title: Ebola Outbreak in Congo Reaches Record Levels as Neighbors Increase Safety Measures
subtitle: Health officials struggle to contain the fast-moving virus while neighboring countries prepare for potential cross-border spread.

summary.en: The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo has now caused at least 930 deaths. This is the fastest-growing outbreak of the virus ever recorded. Health workers are facing dangerous conditions due to local violence and rumors. Neighboring countries like Zambia and Uganda are increasing their border checks to prevent the disease from spreading. International organizations are calling for more funding to help stop the virus.
summary.zh: 剛果民主共和國的伊波拉疫情目前已造成至少九百三十人死亡。這是史上傳播速度最快的伊波拉疫情。由於當地暴力衝突與謠言，醫護人員正處於危險的環境中。尚比亞與烏干達等鄰國正加強邊境檢查，以防止疾病擴散。國際組織正呼籲各界提供更多資金，以協助遏止病毒蔓延。

paragraphs.en: The Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) is currently facing its most serious Ebola crisis on record. According to the country's Ministry of Health, at least 930 people have died since the outbreak began on May 15. The total number of confirmed cases has reached 2,344. Health officials report that the virus is spreading faster than any previous Ebola outbreak, creating a difficult situation for medical teams working in the eastern part of the country.
paragraphs.zh: 剛果民主共和國（Democratic Republic of Congo）目前正遭遇史上最嚴重的伊波拉病毒危機。根據該國衛生部指出，自五月十五日疫情爆發以來，已有至少九百三十人死亡，確診病例總數達到二千三百四十四例。衛生官員表示，該病毒的傳播速度比以往任何一次伊波拉疫情都快，這為在該國東部工作的醫療團隊帶來了艱鉅的挑戰。

paragraphs.en: Ebola is a very serious and often fatal disease. It is highly contagious, meaning it spreads easily from person to person through contact with bodily fluids like blood or vomit. It can also spread through contact with items used by sick people, such as clothing or bedding. The specific virus responsible for this outbreak is known as the Bundibugyo virus. Currently, there is no approved vaccine or treatment for this specific type of the disease.
paragraphs.zh: 伊波拉是一種非常嚴重且往往致命的疾病。它具有高度傳染性，這意味著它能透過接觸血液或嘔吐物等體液，輕易地在人與人之間傳播。它也能透過接觸病患使用過的物品（如衣物或寢具）來傳播。導致此次疫情的特定病毒被稱為邦迪布焦病毒（Bundibugyo virus）。目前，針對此類型的疾病尚無核准的疫苗或治療方法。

paragraphs.en: NPR News reports that the response to the outbreak is being held back by safety concerns. The eastern region of the DRC is currently affected by rebel violence, which makes it hard for doctors and nurses to reach those who need help. There have been at least 12 attacks on health facilities and medical teams. Many of these attacks are driven by rumors and fear among the local population. Furthermore, many health workers have stopped working to protest the fact that they have not been paid. Tragically, 36 health workers have already died after becoming infected with the virus.
paragraphs.zh: 美國國家公共廣播電台（NPR）報導，疫情的應對工作正因安全疑慮而受阻。剛果民主共和國東部地區目前受叛軍暴力影響，導致醫師與護理師難以接觸到需要幫助的民眾。當地已發生至少十二起針對醫療設施與醫療團隊的攻擊事件。這些攻擊多半源於當地居民之間的謠言與恐懼。此外，許多醫護人員因不滿未獲薪資而罷工。令人遺憾的是，已有三十六名醫護人員在感染病毒後不幸殉職。

paragraphs.en: MedPage Today notes that safety fears continue to limit the ability of health teams to provide care. Despite these challenges, the DRC Health Ministry says they are working to improve their response. As of the latest update, 724 patients are in isolation or hospital care. The ministry also reported that 22 people recovered from the virus in a single 24-hour period, showing that some progress is being made in patient support.
paragraphs.zh: 醫學新聞網站 MedPage Today 指出，安全恐懼持續限制了醫療團隊提供照護的能力。儘管面臨這些挑戰，剛果民主共和國衛生部表示，他們正致力於改善應對措施。根據最新數據，有七百二十四名病患正處於隔離或住院治療中。衛生部亦報告稱，在二十四小時內有二十二名病患康復，顯示在病患支援方面已取得部分進展。

paragraphs.en: Because the virus is spreading so quickly, neighboring countries are taking action to protect their own citizens. Deutsche Welle (DW) reports that countries bordering the DRC have increased screening at border crossings and activated emergency plans. In Zambia, authorities have reinforced prevention measures nationwide. Dr. Naeem Dalal, an official with the Zambia National Public Health Institute, explained that they are focusing on infection control, which includes mandatory handwashing and the use of face masks.
paragraphs.zh: 由於病毒傳播速度極快，鄰國正採取行動以保護本國公民。德國之聲（DW）報導，與剛果民主共和國接壤的國家已加強邊境檢查並啟動緊急應變計畫。在尚比亞，當局已在全國範圍內強化預防措施。尚比亞國家公共衛生研究所官員奈姆．達拉爾（Naeem Dalal）醫師解釋，他們正專注於感染控制，包括強制洗手與配戴口罩。

paragraphs.en: Uganda is also increasing surveillance along its border. Dr. Beda Senkware, speaking on national television, identified three districts as being at the highest risk of seeing the disease enter the country. Rwanda has also strengthened its health screening and prepared rapid response teams to investigate any suspected cases. These efforts are part of a larger, coordinated plan by the East African Community to improve regional preparedness and reduce the risk of the virus moving across borders.
paragraphs.zh: 烏干達也正加強邊境監控。貝達．森克瓦雷（Beda Senkware）醫師在全國電視節目中指出，有三個行政區面臨疾病入侵的高風險。盧安達也強化了健康篩檢，並準備了快速反應小組以調查任何疑似病例。這些努力是東非共同體（East African Community）更大規模協調計畫的一部分，旨在提升區域準備能力並降低病毒跨國傳播的風險。

paragraphs.en: Despite these efforts, there is a growing concern that the official numbers do not show the full picture. Experts believe that many cases are going undetected, especially in areas where conflict makes it difficult for health teams to travel. There is also a significant gap in funding for the response. International donors are being asked to provide more than $400 million to support the joint plan between the Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention and other global health partners. As the situation continues to develop, the focus remains on improving surveillance, laboratory testing, and the safety of the brave health workers on the front lines.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管做出了這些努力，外界日益擔憂官方數據未能呈現疫情全貌。專家認為許多病例未被發現，特別是在衝突地區，醫療團隊難以進入。此外，應對資金也存在巨大缺口。國際捐助者被要求提供超過四億美元，以支持非洲疾病管制暨預防中心（Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention）與其他全球衛生夥伴的聯合計畫。隨著局勢持續發展，焦點仍將放在改善監控、實驗室檢測，以及保障前線勇敢醫護人員的安全上。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To be prevented from making progress or being successful.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「退後」之意，這裡指進展受到阻礙。文中：NPR News reports that the response to the outbreak is being held back by safety concerns.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To be caused or motivated by something.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「駕駛」，這裡指行為背後的動機或原因。文中：Many of these attacks are driven by rumors and fear among the local population.

keyTerms.definitionEn: The most active or important position in a difficult situation or conflict.
keyTerms.noteZh: 原指軍事上的前線，這裡比喻在危險環境中直接提供醫療服務的人員。文中：As the situation continues to develop, the focus remains on improving surveillance, laboratory testing, and the safety of the brave health workers on the front lines.

sources.name: NPR News

previousArticles.title: US Parasite Outbreak Linked to Lettuce Grows as Health Officials Face Funding Challenges
previousArticles.summaryEn: A record-breaking outbreak of cyclosporiasis is spreading across the United States. The illness, caused by a parasite, leads to severe stomach issues like diarrhea and vomiting. Taylor Farms has recalled shredded iceberg lettuce in 27 states due to potential contamination. Health experts warn that the response is being slowed by significant cuts to public health funding. Officials expect the number of cases to rise further as investigations continue.

previousArticles.title: US Parasite Outbreak Grows as Officials Struggle with Funding Cuts
previousArticles.summaryEn: The United States is facing a record-breaking outbreak of cyclosporiasis, a parasitic illness causing severe stomach problems. Taylor Farms has issued a recall for shredded iceberg lettuce across 27 states due to potential contamination. Health officials are struggling to manage the crisis following significant federal budget cuts. Many local health departments report that they lack the staff and resources needed to track the disease effectively. The situation remains dangerous as investigators continue to look for other possible sources of the parasite.

previousArticles.title: Europe Faces Growing Health Crisis as Early Heat Waves Spike Death Rates
previousArticles.summaryEn: A recent, intense heat wave across Europe has led to a major increase in deaths. Experts report that over 10,000 more people died than would normally be expected during the hottest week in June. Older adults, particularly those over 65, have been the most affected by these extreme temperatures. Researchers link the rising frequency and strength of these heat waves to climate change. Governments are now working to understand the full scale of this public health challenge.

quiz.question: What is the primary reason given for the difficulty in delivering medical aid in the eastern DRC?

options.A: A lack of available medical supplies and vaccines.
options.B: Safety risks caused by local rebel violence and civil unrest.
options.C: The rapid spread of the virus among medical staff.
options.D: A lack of interest from international health organizations.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，NPR News 指出該地區受到叛軍暴力影響，導致醫療團隊難以進入，且醫療設施已遭受至少 12 次攻擊。選項 A 錯誤，因為雖然提到沒有疫苗，但這不是阻礙醫療團隊進入的主因；選項 C 雖然提到醫護人員感染，但這是結果而非阻礙進入的原因；選項 D 與文意不符，國際組織正在積極籌款。

quiz.question: Based on the information provided, why might the actual number of Ebola cases be higher than the officially reported 2,344?

options.A: The government is intentionally hiding data to prevent public panic.
options.B: Neighboring countries are failing to report cases at their borders.
options.C: Conflict and regional instability prevent health workers from reaching all affected areas.
options.D: The virus has mutated, making it impossible to detect with current testing methods.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據文章最後一段，專家認為官方數據未呈現全貌，主因是衝突地區交通困難，導致許多病例未被檢測到。選項 A 文中未提及蓄意隱瞞；選項 B 提到鄰國正在加強篩檢，並非隱匿疫情；選項 D 文中提到的是目前沒有疫苗，而非檢測技術失效，故選項 C 為最合理的推論。

quiz.question: In the sentence 'The response to the outbreak is being held back by safety concerns,' what does the phrase 'held back' mean?
quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。在此語境下，「held back」意指受到阻礙或延遲。文章提到安全問題（如叛軍攻擊）使得醫護人員難以進入災區，導致防疫反應進度受阻。選項 B 與文意相反；選項 C 過於絕對，因為醫療工作仍在進行中；選項 D 則與安全問題導致的「執行困難」意義不同。

quiz.question: What is the central message of this article?

options.A: The DRC government has successfully eradicated the Ebola virus through new policies.
options.B: Neighboring countries are solely responsible for the current Ebola crisis.
options.C: The Ebola outbreak in the DRC is a complex crisis complicated by conflict, funding gaps, and regional health risks.
options.D: The Bundibugyo virus is the most dangerous virus ever discovered in human history.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。全文綜合討論了疫情嚴重性、衝突導致的救援困難、鄰國的防疫準備以及資金缺口，全面性地概括了當前危機的複雜性。選項 A 與事實不符，疫情仍在發展；選項 B 過於片面，忽略了 DRC 內部的問題；選項 D 在文中並未將此病毒與歷史上所有病毒進行比較，僅說明其嚴重性。
